item.title: U.S. carries out more Iran strikes as Trump threatens "major military punishment"
item.description: The U.S. moves more refueling planes to the Mideast and adds medics at a major military hospital as the war with Iran spreads to another key waterway.

item.title: Trump imposes new tariffs on dozens of countries, citing forced labor concerns
item.description: The Trump administration will impose tariffs of up to 12.5% on goods from 60 U.S. trading partners accused of failing to crack down on forced labor, extending the administration's tariffs on much of the world.

item.title: Man shot and killed by police in Madison, Wisconsin, sparking protests
item.description: Madison's chief of police says an officer in Wisconsin's capital city shot and killed a man who had injured him with a knife while resisting arrest.

item.title: Member of Vance's Secret Service detail under investigation for leaking
item.description: The Secret Service said a member of Vice President JD Vance's detail is under investigation for "compromising operational and information security."

item.title: Rapid pace of interceptor and precision-guided weapons use worries Trump admin.
item.description: The U.S. defense industrial base isn't equipped to produce missiles rapidly enough to replenish munitions used in the war with Iran, or transferred for use to Central Command.

item.title: Trump fires U.S. attorney in Washington on heels of court appointment
item.description: Judges on the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Washington had appointed Roger Rogoff as U.S. attorney in Seattle. But he was fired shortly after.

item.title: Bertha weakens to tropical depression after dumping rain along Gulf Coast
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: Full contents of first Nancy Guthrie ransom note revealed
item.description: Nearly six months after Nancy Guthrie was taken from her home in Arizona, the full contents of a ransom note that might have come from her kidnapper are coming to light. Mark Strassmann reports on the latest developments in the search for the mother of "Today" show host Savannah Guthrie.

item.title: U.S. attorney who served for less than an hour suing Trump administration over firing
item.description: A U.S. attorney who served for less than an hour is suing the Trump administration over his firing. Roger Rogoff is asking a federal court to reinstate him as U.S. Attorney for the Western District of Washington. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson breaks down the legal questions in this case.

item.title: 1.6 million eggs are recalled over potential salmonella contamination
item.description: The recall affects white-shell and cage-free eggs sold under multiple grocery brands in six states. No illnesses have been reported.

item.title: How Taco Bell is responding to the cyclospora outbreak
item.description: U.S. health officials are investigating a new outbreak of the parasite cyclospora that's linked to an unspecified product. Meanwhile, Taco Bell is reporting a major drop in foot traffic as concerns about fresh produce continue. Carter Evans has the latest update and Heather Haddon, a reporter for the Wall Street Journal, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Some Trump officials concerned about rapid pace of missile usage in Iran, sources say
item.description: The U.S. military is expanding its presence in the Middle East with the arrival of new equipment, as sources say concerns are growing within the Trump administration over rapid use of defensive interceptors and long-range missiles. Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: FDA advisory panel recommends loosening restrictions on 2 unapproved peptides
item.description: An FDA advisory panel recommended easing restrictions on certain peptides on Thursday. Peptides are short chains of amino acids that can help regulate a variety of functions in the body. CBS News correspondent Adam Yamaguchi reports.

item.title: How to save on your next laptop as AI drives up prices
item.description: As the back-to-school shopping season begins, you can expect to pay more for certain items, like laptops, due to shortages stemming from artificial intelligence. Brian Westover, principal hardware writer for PCMag, joins CBS News to discuss how you can save money.

item.title: The major contenders vying for Senate seat to replace Lindsey Graham
item.description: Mark Sanford, former South Carolina governor and congressman, has added his name to the list of Republican candidates hoping to succeed the late Lindsey Graham in the U.S. Senate. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more details.

item.title: Vance Boelter gets 2 life terms and more in Minnesota lawmaker shootings
item.description: Vance Boelter, the man who admitted in court to shooting two Minnesota lawmakers and their spouses, was sentenced to two life terms in prison followed by 40 years Thursday morning.

item.title: Dad, son on mission to play catch every single day for a year
item.description: A father-son duo in Idaho is on a mission to play catch every single day for a year, no matter the weather, and they're nearing the finish line. Tony Dokoupil reports.

item.title: Texas appeals court reverses injunction that closed midwife's clinics
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton accused Maria Rojas of performing illegal abortions and practicing medicine without a license last year.

item.title: Trump imposes new tariffs on dozens of countries over forced labor concerns
item.description: President Trump is imposing new tariffs on dozens of countries accused of failing to crack down on forced labor. CBS News politics reporter Kathryn Watson has the latest.

item.title: U.S. launches 13th straight night of strikes on Iran
item.description: The U.S. on Thursday launched a 13th straight night of strikes against Iran after President Trump threatened Iran and the Houthis with "major military punishment." Retired Marine Col. Mark Cancian joins with analysis.

item.title: 25 states sue FEMA and DHS, accuse Trump admin. of withholding funding
item.description: The lawsuit claims the Trump administration is withholding hundreds of millions of dollars in disaster-preparedness and homeland security grants to pressure states to change policies on elections and immigration.

item.title: FDA says new cyclospora outbreak linked to unspecified product
item.description: Federal health officials said they've identified a new outbreak of the cyclospora parasite, but the source hasn't been specified.

item.title: House rebukes Trump over Iran war, but similar war powers effort stalls in the Senate
item.description: The House on Thursday passed a measure to limit President Trump's wartime powers, reflecting negative sentiment around Iran. A similar measure was struck down in the Senate hours later. CBS News' Taurean Small reports.

item.title: Former DNC Chair Jaime Harrison makes his case for keeping South Carolina as first 2028 primary
item.description: The debate over Democrats' 2028 primary calendar has gotten contentious, pitting those who want South Carolina to be first against those who want Nevada. Jaime Harrison, former Democratic National Committee chair, joins with his perspective.

item.title: 7/23: CBS Evening News
item.description: Outrage over a deadly police shooting in Wisconsin; two people were stabbed near New York's Central Park.

item.title: 7/23: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump tells Axios he's considering a "massive attack" on Iran; GOP House Judiciary chair issues criminal referral against Jack Smith.

item.title: Measles outbreak "slowing," but newborn's family still can't meet her
item.description: Utah's weekly measles case count has fallen. The national tally hasn't, and a newborn who's too young to be vaccinated is caught in between.

item.title: Malcolm-Jamal Warner's widow on actor's death: "My body went through shock"
item.description: Tenisha Warner, the widow of "The Cosby Show" actor Malcolm-Jamal Warner, spoke exclusively to "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King about coping with grief one year after his death.

item.title: Tenisha Warner, widow of Malcolm-Jamal Warner, opens up about being a single mom
item.description: Tenisha Warner sat down for an exclusive interview with "CBS Mornings" before a lawsuit over her late husband's estate became public, and discussed her grief and raising her daughter alone.

item.title: Introducing Norah O'Donnell's new health podcast
item.description: "Healthful with Norah O'Donnell" launches July 22.
